channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin will talk peace while continuing to fight: Wesley Clark
item.description: Ukrainian leaders have already agreed to a 30-day halt in fighting, and now, U.S. diplomats are seeking Russia’s acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Kuwait frees 10 jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released 10 American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies. “President Trump has made it a priority to bring home Americans detained overseas.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: UN-backed experts accuse Israel of sexual and gender-based violence in Gaza
item.description: A United Nations-backed group of human rights experts accused Israel of “disproportionate violence against women and children” during the war against Hamas in Gaza, and accused Israeli security forces of rape and sexual violence against Palestinian detainees.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN: Sudan war is world's worst humanitarian crisis: 30 million need aid, 16 million of them kids
item.description: The head of the U.N. children's agency says the war in Sudan has created the world’s largest and most devastating humanitarian crisis, with over 30 million people needing aid this year – 16 million of them children.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin thanks Trump for principles of ceasefire push, but does not say yes
item.description: Russian President Putin says he agrees in "principle" with President Trump's desire for a 30-day ceasefire but argued it would only help Ukraine and said he wants a lasting peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu slams 'terrorist-supporting' UN council that accused Israel of committing sexual crimes
item.description: Israeli and American officials are condemning a report from the United Nations Human Rights Council that accuses Israel of carrying out systemic acts of sexual violence against Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steve Witkoff has gone from spotting Trump cash at a deli to helping him navigate Ukraine and Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump special envoy Steve Witkoff arrived in Moscow on Thursday for high-level talks aimed at ending Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the latest high-profile assignment for the real estate magnate turned White House foreign policy fixer.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: ADDIS ABABA, Ethiopia (AP) — The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People's Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL GETACHEW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: President Duda calls on US to place nuclear weapons in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's President Andrzej Duda again called on the United States to deploy nuclear weapons to Poland as a deterrent to Russia, the latest indication that the frontline NATO nation is increasingly considering nuclear protection as fears of Russia grow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top diplomats from G7 countries meet in Canada as Trump threatens more tariffs on US allies
item.description: Top diplomats from Group of 7 have held a first day of talks in Canada as U.S. President Donald Trump's trade and foreign policies have thrown the bloc's once solid unity into disarray.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Argentine retirees and soccer fans clash with police during protests against austerity measures
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentine retirees and fans of several soccer teams clashed with police Wednesday during a protest in front of Congress in the capital, Buenos Aires, against the economic policies implemented by the government of President Javier Milei.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lutnick: Canada becoming US state 'best way' to merge economies
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Wednesday that Canada becoming a U.S. state is the “best way” to merge the two countries’ economies. “The best way, the president has said it, the best way to actually merge the economies of Canada and the United States is for Canada to become our 51st state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Pressed to accept a ceasefire in Ukraine, Putin seems likely to seek his own conditions
item.description: By signaling its openness to a ceasefire, Ukraine has handed the Kremlin a difficult challenge at a time when the Russian military has the upper hand in the war: Should Moscow accept a truce and abandon hopes of making new gains, or should it reject the offer and risk derailing a cautious rapprochem
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria's new government is stopping family reunions immediately for migrants
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The new Austrian government said Wednesday that family reunion procedures for migrants will be immediately halted because the country is no longer able to absorb newcomers adequately.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and PHILIPP JENNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine launches biggest drone attack on Moscow, killing 2, as US talks begin
item.description: Ukraine launched its largest-ever drone attack on Moscow as a delegation met with U.S. officials in Saudi Arabia for talks about ending the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Scotland golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: A pro-Palestinian activist group has claimed responsibility for the crimes, which included defacing a building and digging up greens.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation
